Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint To Add Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. As A Defendant
Off Calendar Jul-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Glassman, Esq. 310-477-1700 of Panish Shea & Boyle LLP for Plaintiff BENJAMIN ROSSINI through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Plaintiff submits to the tentative rulings. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Notice And Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Jul-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Glassman, Esq. 310-477-1700 of Panish Shea & Boyle LLP for Plaintiff BENJAMIN ROSSINI through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Plaintiff submits to the tentative rulings. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AVATAR FOOD INT'L, INC., Motion To Change Venue.

Defendant Avatar Food International, Inc.'s motion to change venue based upon the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice is GRANTED. The court transfers this action to Santa Clara County and Avatar shall pay all required transfer fees. In determining whether the ends of justice will be promoted in transferring an action, "[t]he trial court may rely not only on the direct facts set forth in the affidavits, but also on any reasonable and relevant inference arising therefrom." (J.C. Millett Co. v. Latchford-Marble Glass Co. (1959) 167 Cal.App.2d 218, 227.) Here, the undisputed Roberts declaration demonstrates that the accident occurred in Santa Clara County, plaintiff received his medical care in Santa Clara County, and the primary investigators of the accident are located in Santa Clara County. The court can reasonably infer that most, if not all, of the key non-party witnesses are located at or near that county. In addition, Contreras v. Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. (18CV328362) is a related case that arises from the same accident. A transfer is necessary for potential consolidation of the two matters should the Santa Clara County Court so order. Consolidation may well preserve judicial and party resources and prevent duplicative work and inconsistent rulings.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
